projects.name: Pull Starboard Propeller & Shaft
projects.notes:
I have put this forward to Todd at Pacific Marine Center as a working outline of expenses for the worst case scenario. Hopefully we can pull the propeller and shaft, repair the shaft and re-install. 
I am waiting for a response from him. Sounds like he went in for knee surgery this week. 

WORST CASE SCENARIO
4-6 hours to pull the propeller and the shaft, the rudders don't interfere with the shaft removal. 
3-5 thousand for a shaft if needed. 
6-8 hours for replacing a cutlass bearing. 
4-6 hours for installing new shaft and propeller. 

    24hrs labor @ $145/hr = $4,000 with tax, 
   $6,000 materials with tax.
Estimate of safe worst case scenario ~$10,000: 

GUNING FOR 
4-8 hours to pull the propeller and the shaft, the rudders don't interfere with the shaft removal. 
$900 for shaft repair. 
4-6 hours for installing new shaft and propeller. 

  14 hours labor @ 145/hr = $2,000
  Shaft repair $900
$3,200 with tax

scopeTasks.desc: Managing Project

GUNING FOR 
4-8 hours to pull the propeller and the shaft, the rudders don't interfere with the shaft removal. 
$1,000 for shaft repair. 
4-6 hours for installing new shaft and propeller. 

  14 hours labor @ 145/hr = $2,000
  Shaft repair $1,000
$3,200 with tax

projects.name: Oil Pressure Gauges Alarming
projects.notes:
Functional to AK and back through the Fall. 
New sending units will be tested on her trip to Anacortes for haul out. My sense is that the present location of these exposes them to too much heat. If the new sending units resolve the issue as expected we can relocate the sending units where they will not be exposed to the heat exposure right there next to the engine block.

**Will follow up with Change of Scope when we are clear that the new sending units work.
